rationale: Title Y is stronger for PACE because it states the actual comparison, highlights the main clinical findings, and gives the cohort size. It is more specific and more likely to attract electrophysiology readers. Title X is clear and journal-appropriate, but it stays generic and does not convey the key result.
loser_weakness: Title X lacks the comparator group, the main findings, and the sample size, making it less informative and less compelling despite good clarity.
